description: Following the collapse of Iceland's three major banks in October 2008, the government faced massive pressure to accept the 'Icesave' debt repayment deal to the UK and Netherlands. The public strongly opposed using taxpayer money to cover private bank losses, leading to the 2010 and 2011 referendums where the populace overwhelmingly rejected the repayment terms.

key_events.description: Collapse of Glitnir, Landsbanki, and Kaupthing banks triggers national emergency.
UK government uses anti-terrorism legislation to freeze Icelandic assets.
President Grímsson refuses to sign the Icesave bill, forcing a national referendum.
Voters reject the repayment deal in a landslide vote.

calibration_notes: Excellent for measuring extreme public pushback against state-backed bailouts of private financial entities.
